title: Educators balk as Merrimack, Suffolk plan to roll out 3-year bachelor’s degrees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Merrimack College and Suffolk University plan a three-year bachelor's degree pilot starting fall 2027.
The pilot programs offer 94-96 credits, fewer than the standard 120, targeting cost and time reduction.
Massachusetts Teachers Association fears the programs could deepen inequities and lower education quality.

title: HGTV’s Taniya Nayak talks designing a Bruin’s home and new Boston restaurants
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Taniya Nayak received an honorary doctorate from Boston Architectural College and continues to expand her design projects.
Nayak co-owns several Massachusetts and Rhode Island restaurants and designed the Newton home of Patrice Bergeron.
Upcoming projects include Buitoni Spaghetti Bar in Kenmore Square and a lighting line with Studio M.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (July 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington's most expensive home sale was 451 Lincoln St., a raised ranch built in 1969, sold for $875,000.
A home on 709 Main St. in Acton, a 4,210-square-foot Colonial built in 2024, sold for $1,550,000.
In Belmont, 21 Wellington Lane sold as the highest-priced property at $3,100,000 for a 3,798-square-foot Colonial.

title: Robert Parish: Talk of Celtics trading Jaylen Brown is ‘disturbing’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Robert Parish criticized Celtics management for considering trading Jaylen Brown, calling it a serious miscalculation by the team.
Brown was previously linked to a failed trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo; Celtics continue to weigh offers after playoff exit.
Celtics' asking price for Jaylen Brown includes up to four first-round picks and players, per The Athletic's Sam Amick.

title: State Police sergeant, former lieutenant under criminal investigation for alleged coverup in wake of fatal crash, court documents reveal
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police Sgt. Mark Delaney and former Lt. Anthony DeLucia are under criminal investigation for their alleged roles in a cover-up.
The investigation is tied to Sgt. Scott Quigley's fatal 2023 crash while allegedly intoxicated, which resulted in the death of Angelo Schettino.
Quigley's involvement has delayed the Phan brothers' murder trial, as his legal issues have raised concerns about witness credibility.

title: 5 players Bruins should target in free agency on Wednesday
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins are targeting five players in free agency to bolster defense and scoring options, including Ryan Shea and Matias Maccelli.
Ryan Shea is a 29-year-old defenseman from Pittsburgh, noted for his 5-on-5 play and outlet passing skills.
Jacob Trouba is considered as a potential short-term defensive option to strengthen Bruins' second pairing.

title: Watch: Red Sox, Nationals brawl after Willson Contreras charges mound
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras charged the mound against Nationals pitcher Cade Cavalli after being shouted at post-strikeout.
Three players, including Contreras, were ejected following a bench-clearing altercation at Fenway Park.
Nationals won 8-1 with Cade Cavalli striking out 13 Red Sox players over seven innings.

title: Person shot and killed, suspect also dead after incident that closed I-495 in Lowell for hours, officials say
author: Darin Zullo and Samantha Genzer
bullets: I-495 in Lowell was closed after a suspect shot a person, then died from self-inflicted wounds during a standoff.
Berlin police responded to a shooting at Highland Plaza; the victim died at the scene from multiple gunshot wounds.
The suspect and victim knew each other; police shattered the window to access the suspect's vehicle with non-lethal rounds.

title: Dunkin’ drops new ‘Eagle Cup’ for America’s 250th
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Dunkin' launched a $10.99 'Eagle Cup' featuring a bald eagle head design in celebration of America's 250th anniversary.
The cup includes a free medium beverage and a code for 30 days of $3 Dunkin' drinks, expiring July 31.
Available nationwide for a limited time, each store may have only 20 cups, as supply varies by location.

title: NBA insider: Celtics must ‘significantly’ lower asking price in Jaylen Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Celtics are asking for up to four first-round picks and players in a trade for Jaylen Brown.
Jaylen Brown is reportedly frustrated with the Celtics' trade approach after a strong season and MVP consideration.
Portland and Denver show limited interest in Brown, with the Nets not engaged in trade talks with the Celtics.
